justification: All standard ADR sections present (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered). Crash-recovery lock behavior explicitly and thoroughly described in Consequences (zombie connection, delayed next run). All three alternatives listed (Redis SETNX, ZooKeeper, heartbeat table). Context clearly explains the multi-node expansion problem. No invented information. ADR is readable as a standalone document.

observations.text: Excellent crash-recovery analysis covering both normal connection drop and zombie connection edge case
All three alternatives listed with clear rejection rationale in a well-formatted table
Context section thoroughly explains why coordination is needed (duplicate runs, data corruption, conflicting writes)
Includes useful compliance notes about lock key management and monitoring — adds operational value without inventing information
Separated Rationale from Decision, which improves readability beyond the minimum requirement
Consequences split into positive and negative sections with clear trade-off framing

agent_output_excerpt:
# ADR-001: PostgreSQL Advisory Locks for Distributed Batch Job Coordination

**Status:** Accepted
**Date:** 2026-03-18
**Deciders:** MJ (Systems Architect)

---

## Context

The nightly reconciliation job previously ran on a single node with no coordination mechanism. As the system expands to multiple nodes, a distributed coordination strategy is required to ensure exactly one node executes the batch job per night. Without coordination, concurrent execution across nodes would produce duplicate r
